ops.title: go-git pack index is a one-shot snapshot — gitobj object reads must go through retry/lookupCommit
ops.body: go-git's filesystem.ObjectStorage seeds its packfile index on the first pack-touching read and never rescans objects/pack (Reindex() is the only invalidation; upstream go-git#1623 closed not-planned, go-git#2242 open). Any new object-read call site in internal/gitobj must route through retry()/lookupCommit() so a pack landed by an external git fetch/repack/gc self-heals via reindex-and-retry — a bare object.Get* call reintroduces the transient 'incomplete chain' / silently-truncated-walk bug (task 519097e, fixed in b93175ae). Loose-object reads are never stale in our non-ExclusiveAccess config; only pack-index reads are.
